name: Eino
tagline: LLM application and agent framework for Go, built the Golang way
seoTitle: Eino: Go LLM & AI Agent Framework
metaDescription: Eino is an open-source Golang framework for LLM apps and AI agents. Build with reusable components, graph composition, streaming, and an Agent Development Kit.
overview: Eino is an LLM application development framework written in Go. It draws on ideas from LangChain, Google ADK, and similar projects, but follows Golang conventions so the code feels natural to Go developers.

It gives you reusable building blocks like ChatModel, Tool, Retriever, and ChatTemplate, with official implementations for providers such as OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and Ollama. You can wire these blocks into graphs and workflows, or use the Agent Development Kit (ADK) to build agents that call tools and coordinate with each other.

Eino is part of the CloudWeGo open-source family. The core repo holds the type definitions, streaming engine, component abstractions, and agent implementations, while separate repos provide ready-made component implementations, dev tooling, and examples.
features: Component abstractions (ChatModel, Tool, Retriever, Embedding, and more) with official implementations for OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, Ark, Ollama, and Elasticsearch
Agent Development Kit (ADK) for tool use, multi-agent coordination, context management, and ready-to-use agent patterns
Graph and workflow composition with precise control over execution flow, and the option to expose a graph as a tool for agents
DeepAgent that breaks complex tasks into steps, delegates to sub-agents, and can run shell commands, execute Python, and search the web
Automatic stream processing that concatenates, merges, and copies streams as data flows between nodes
Interrupt and resume support so any agent or tool can pause for human input and continue from a checkpoint

gettingStarted.intro: Eino is a Go module that needs Go 1.18 or above. Add it to your project, then configure a ChatModel and wrap it in an agent.

steps.heading: Install Eino
steps.body: Add the Eino module to your Go project.

steps.heading: Build a ChatModelAgent
steps.body: Configure a ChatModel, build an agent around it, and run a query through a Runner. The agent handles the ReAct loop internally.

steps.heading: Compose a graph
steps.body: When you need exact control over flow, use compose to connect nodes into a graph, then compile and invoke it.
steps.code:
graph := compose.NewGraph[*Input, *Output]()
graph.AddLambdaNode("validate", validateFn)
graph.AddChatModelNode("generate", chatModel)
graph.AddEdge(compose.START, "validate")
graph.AddEdge("validate", "generate")
graph.AddEdge("generate", compose.END)

runnable, _ := graph.Compile(ctx)
result, _ := runnable.Invoke(ctx, input)

useCases: Building tool-using AI agents in Go that decide when to call tools and when to respond
Coordinating multiple specialized agents on complex tasks with DeepAgent, including running shell commands, Python code, and web search
Composing deterministic LLM workflows as graphs and exposing them as tools for autonomous agents
Adding human-in-the-loop steps where an agent pauses for input and resumes from a checkpoint
Instrumenting LLM apps with logging, tracing, and metrics through callback aspects
